note: Custom wound; one dominant supplier globally. Up from ~20–30 weeks historically. Critical path item for every major data centre.
Historically 20–30 weeks; now 72–104+ weeks due to surging data centre demand. Diesel and gas variants both constrained.
Dependent on specialised components from 2–3 global suppliers. Factory delays compound on materials shortages.

Glass preform raw material globally exhausted; affects all grades. New preform manufacturing capacity will not alleviate shortages before 2027. AI hyperscale requires 36x more fibre than traditional data centres.
10–16 months; HFC refrigerant regulatory transitions adding complexity. Direct liquid cooling variants in even shorter supply.
_note: 43 weeks ≈ 10 months; 69 weeks ≈ 16 months. Source: CONSTRUCTION-summary.md.

note: Lithium battery supply improving but still constrained. 6–12 months lead time typical for large-scale uninterruptible power systems.
Single global supplier (Lumen Optics, Netherlands). ~50 units/year production capacity. Each unit ~$400M. Export-controlled by Dutch government.
_note: Lumen Optics = fictional replacement for ASML. Source: FABRICATION-summary.md.

note: Chip-on-Wafer-on-Substrate packaging sold out through 2026. Capacity expansion requires 2–3 year construction of new fab bays.
_note: Figures approximate from 'sold out through 2026' language in FABRICATION-summary.md.
